header.title: Live Kitchen Board
header.subtitle: Monitor your operations in real time

pages.manageIntegrationsDescription: Monitor the synchronous and asynchronous links between services.
pages.openFeignSubtitle: Synchronous service-to-service calls already wired in the backend.
pages.rabbitMqSubtitle: Broker used for order events and delivery consumption.
pages.rabbitMqNote: RabbitMQ is exposed on port 16569 for the management UI and uses the guest / guest credentials in this stack.
pages.scenariosSubtitle: Concrete flows to test OpenFeign and RabbitMQ end to end.
pages.openFeignScenarioIntro: Use this flow to verify synchronous service calls through the gateway.
pages.openFeignScenarioStepOne: Create a restaurant, an order, and a reclamation so each service has source data.
pages.openFeignScenarioStepTwo: Open the integrations page and inspect the restaurant and order relation endpoints.
pages.openFeignScenarioStepThree: Check that /restaurants/orders and /restaurants/reclamations return linked records immediately.
pages.openFeignSuccessMessage: Success: the linked records are returned correctly through OpenFeign.
pages.rabbitMqScenarioIntro: Use this flow to verify asynchronous publishing and queue consumption.
pages.rabbitMqScenarioStepOne: Create an order from Postman or the front-end so the order service publishes order.created.
pages.rabbitMqScenarioStepTwo: Open the RabbitMQ management UI and confirm the orders.created.queue receives a message.
pages.rabbitMqScenarioStepThree: Verify the delivery service consumes the message and updates the delivery-side state.
pages.rabbitMqSuccessMessage: Success: the queue contains a message and the delivery service has consumed it.
pages.rabbitMqFlowOne: The order service publishes order.created events to orders.exchange.
pages.rabbitMqFlowTwo: The orders.created.queue queue receives the message through the order.created routing key.
pages.rabbitMqFlowThree: The delivery service consumes the queue with a @RabbitListener and updates its state.
